---
title: database/sql Driver
description: Typed query functions for Go's standard database/sql package.
---

import { Tabs, TabItem } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';

The `database-sql` driver generates typed Go functions that work with any `*sql.DB` or `*sql.Tx` through a shared `DBTX` interface.

---

## Configuration

```toml
[[targets]]
language = "go"
schema   = "sql/schema.sql"
out      = "internal/db"
driver   = "database-sql"
```

---

## Generated files

| File | Contents |
|------|----------|
| `client.go` | `DBTX` interface, `Queries` struct, `New` constructor |
| `<name>.queries.go` | One file per `.sql` query file, containing typed functions |

---

## client.go

sqlcx always generates this file:

```go
// Code generated by sqlcx. DO NOT EDIT.
package db

import (
	"context"
	"database/sql"
)

type DBTX interface {
	ExecContext(ctx context.Context, query string, args ...interface{}) (sql.Result, error)
	QueryContext(ctx context.Context, query string, args ...interface{}) (*sql.Rows, error)
	QueryRowContext(ctx context.Context, query string, args ...interface{}) *sql.Row
}

type Queries struct {
	db DBTX
}

func New(db DBTX) *Queries {
	return &Queries{db: db}
}
```

`DBTX` is satisfied by both `*sql.DB` and `*sql.Tx`, so you can pass either without changing your application code.

---

## Query annotations

Add annotations to your `.sql` files to control what is generated:

| Annotation | Returns | Use case |
|-----------|---------|----------|
| `:one` | `(*Row, error)` | Fetch a single optional row |
| `:many` | `([]Row, error)` | Fetch a list of rows |
| `:exec` | `error` | Insert / update / delete |
| `:execresult` | `(*Result, error)` | Mutation + rows affected count |

---

## Full example

**SQL queries** (`sql/queries/users.sql`):

```sql
-- name: GetUser :one
SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = $1;

-- name: ListUsers :many
SELECT id, name, email FROM users WHERE name ILIKE $1;

-- name: CreateUser :exec
INSERT INTO users (name, email, bio) VALUES ($1, $2, $3);

-- name: DeleteUser :execresult
DELETE FROM users WHERE id = $1;
```

**Generated** (`users.queries.go`):

```go
// Code generated by sqlcx. DO NOT EDIT.
package db

import (
	"context"
	"database/sql"
	"time"
)

const getUserSQL = "SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = $1"

type GetUserRow struct {
	Id        int64     `db:"id"         json:"id"`
	Name      string    `db:"name"       json:"name"`
	Email     string    `db:"email"      json:"email"`
	Bio       *string   `db:"bio"        json:"bio"`
	Status    string    `db:"status"     json:"status"`
	Tags      []string  `db:"tags"       json:"tags"`
	CreatedAt time.Time `db:"created_at" json:"created_at"`
}

func (q *Queries) GetUser(ctx context.Context, id int64) (*GetUserRow, error) {
	row := q.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, getUserSQL, id)
	var i GetUserRow
	err := row.Scan(&i.Id, &i.Name, &i.Email, &i.Bio, &i.Status, &i.Tags, &i.CreatedAt)
	if err == sql.ErrNoRows {
		return nil, nil
	}
	if err != nil {
		return nil, err
	}
	return &i, nil
}

const listUsersSQL = "SELECT id, name, email FROM users WHERE name ILIKE $1"

type ListUsersRow struct {
	Id    int64  `db:"id"    json:"id"`
	Name  string `db:"name"  json:"name"`
	Email string `db:"email" json:"email"`
}

func (q *Queries) ListUsers(ctx context.Context, name string) ([]ListUsersRow, error) {
	rows, err := q.db.QueryContext(ctx, listUsersSQL, name)
	if err != nil {
		return nil, err
	}
	defer rows.Close()
	var items []ListUsersRow
	for rows.Next() {
		var i ListUsersRow
		if err := rows.Scan(&i.Id, &i.Name, &i.Email); err != nil {
			return nil, err
		}
		items = append(items, i)
	}
	return items, rows.Err()
}

const createUserSQL = "INSERT INTO users (name, email, bio) VALUES ($1, $2, $3)"

func (q *Queries) CreateUser(ctx context.Context, name string, email string, bio string) error {
	_, err := q.db.ExecContext(ctx, createUserSQL, name, email, bio)
	return err
}

const deleteUserSQL = "DELETE FROM users WHERE id = $1"

type DeleteUserResult struct {
	RowsAffected int64
}

func (q *Queries) DeleteUser(ctx context.Context, id int64) (*DeleteUserResult, error) {
	result, err := q.db.ExecContext(ctx, deleteUserSQL, id)
	if err != nil {
		return nil, err
	}
	affected, err := result.RowsAffected()
	if err != nil {
		return nil, err
	}
	return &DeleteUserResult{RowsAffected: affected}, nil
}
```

---

## Using the generated code

```go
package main

import (
	"context"
	"database/sql"
	"fmt"
	"log"

	_ "github.com/lib/pq"
	"myapp/internal/db"
)

func main() {
	conn, err := sql.Open("postgres", "postgres://localhost/myapp?sslmode=disable")
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}

	q := db.New(conn)
	ctx := context.Background()

	// Insert a user
	if err := q.CreateUser(ctx, "Alice", "alice@example.com", ""); err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}

	// Fetch a single user — returns nil if not found
	user, err := q.GetUser(ctx, 1)
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}
	if user != nil {
		fmt.Println(user.Name, user.Email)
	}

	// Fetch a list
	users, err := q.ListUsers(ctx, "%alice%")
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}
	for _, u := range users {
		fmt.Println(u.Id, u.Email)
	}

	// Delete and check how many rows were removed
	res, err := q.DeleteUser(ctx, 1)
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}
	fmt.Printf("deleted %d row(s)\n", res.RowsAffected)
}
```

---

## Using with transactions

Because `New` accepts a `DBTX`, you can pass a `*sql.Tx` for transactional operations:

```go
tx, err := conn.BeginTx(ctx, nil)
if err != nil {
	return err
}
defer tx.Rollback()

q := db.New(tx)
if err := q.CreateUser(ctx, "Bob", "bob@example.com", ""); err != nil {
	return err
}
return tx.Commit()
```
